_comment: Run this with `pnpm test:mutate`, not `stryker run` directly.
Mutation testing reruns the suite once per mutant. The two GPU-backed
layers launch a browser and a graphics device, which takes the suite from
well under a second to about ten — untenable across thousands of mutants.
The script sets RMSL_SKIP_GPU to turn both off. Stryker has no way to set
an environment variable from this file, so running the binary directly
pays that cost on every mutant.
With them off the score measures the substring assertions alone. Since
the real compilers are what caught most of the actual bugs, treat a
surviving mutant as a question — would validation have killed it? —
